message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Task: add optional trans-tag metadata (note/created/modified) parsed in InternationalizationExtension.parse() and stored as node.trans_metadata in _make_node(). Only file I need to touch is src/jinja2/ext.py. What does your feature need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: My task: add domain= parameter support to trans tag in InternationalizationExtension (parse() and _make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py). This is the only file I need to touch. What file(s) does your feature need? If it also touches ext.py, let's figure out how to split edits within the same file (e.g. sequential patches to different methods) or coordinate order of edits.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both need parse() and _make_node() in the same file - can't split by file. Proposal: sequential handoff instead. I'll go first and implement domain= support now (small, isolated change: parse domain kwarg, thread through _make_node, add dgettext/dngettext to _install_callables). I'll send DONE with a summary of exactly which lines/blocks I touched. Wait for my DONE before you start editing, then layer your note/created/modified metadata changes on top and adjust if there's any overlap (I don't expect much - my changes are additive around the trimmed/pluralize keyword parsing and the gettext call generation). Sound OK? Reply ACCEPT and I'll begin immediately.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both only need src/jinja2/ext.py and both touch parse()/_make_node(), so true file-disjointness isn't possible. Propose serialization instead: I'll implement my metadata (note/created/modified) feature first since it's purely additive (new elif branch in the param-parsing loop + one extra optional param in _make_node that only sets an attr when non-empty). I'll keep edits scoped and minimal, run tests, then send DONE. You then pull the updated file and layer your domain= support on top, adjusting the _make_node signature/call site as needed. Sound good? Reply ACCEPT and I'll start immediately; otherwise propose the reverse order.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed - you go first with metadata (note/created/modified), send DONE when finished, then I'll layer domain= support on top of your changes and adjust as needed. Will wait for your DONE before touching the file.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed - you go first with domain= support, send DONE when finished, I'll wait and then layer metadata (note/created/modified) support on top.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Our ACCEPTs crossed in transit and now contradict (mine said you go first, yours said I go first). Let's settle definitively: I will go first (implement domain= now), confirmed file untouched as of now. Please do NOT edit ext.py until you receive my DONE message. Reply ACCEPT one final time to confirm, but I will proceed after a short wait regardless since the file is currently unmodified.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We crossed messages - to resolve unambiguously: I (agent2) will go first now with metadata (note/created/modified) support since your last ACCEPT said 'you go first'. I'll implement, test, and send DONE with a precise summary of every block/line range I touched in parse() and _make_node(). Please hold off editing ext.py until you see my DONE.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Final: you go first with domain=. I will not touch ext.py until I receive your DONE. Waiting now.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We keep racing on timing-based ordering. Final tiebreaker: agent1 goes first (alphabetical/numeric agent-id order, deterministic regardless of message timing). File is currently unmodified. I am starting my domain= edit now. Please hold off until you receive my DONE message with the exact diff/summary. Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Finished domain= support. Blocks I touched in src/jinja2/ext.py: (1) added _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext helper functions right after _make_new_npgettext (~line 245-280); (2) InternationalizationExtension._install: added dgettext/dngettext getattr lookups and passed them to _install_callables; (3) _install_callables: added dgettext/dngettext params, newstyle wrapping, and a fallback (falls back to plain gettext/ngettext if translations obj lacks dgettext/dngettext), added to globals.update(...); (4) _uninstall: added dgettext/dngettext to the pop-key tuple; (5) parse(): added 'domain: t.Optional[str] = None' local var and a new 'if token.value == "domain":' branch inside the 'if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":' block (parses domain="literal", validates it's a Const str, raises TemplateAssertionError on duplicate or non-string), then passes domain as a new positional arg to self._make_node(...); (6) _make_node(): added domain param, branches to build dgettext/dngettext Call nodes instead of gettext/ngettext when domain is set. All 830 existing tests pass. File is now free for you to layer your note/created/modified metadata feature on top - your changes to parse()'s param-parsing loop and _make_node() should be additive alongside mine (different elif branches / an extra optional param), shouldn't structurally conflict. Let me know if you want me to look at the merged diff once you're done.
